Tests that accessing an out-of-bounds index in an array returns undefined.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the behavior of JavaScript arrays when accessing an index that is out of bounds. It initializes an array `a` with a length of 5 and then uses an `assert` statement to check that accessing the element at index 10 returns `undefined`. This illustrates that JavaScript arrays do not throw an error when accessing indices beyond their current length, but instead return `undefined`. The script provides narrow functionality focused on this specific behavior of JavaScript arrays.
# Global Variables

---
### a
- **Type**: ``Array``
- **Description**: An instance of the `Array` object with an initial length of 5. The array is created using the `new Array(5)` constructor, which sets the length property of the array to 5, but does not assign any values to the elements.
- **Use**: Used to demonstrate that accessing an index beyond the initialized length of the array returns `undefined`.
